# Test converting cmd2mcpserver itself to an MCP server

# Create a minimal cmd2mcpserver-like binary for testing
cp cmd2mcpserver.go main.go
exec go build -o cmd2mcpserver main.go
! stderr .

# Run cmd2mcpserver on itself with dry-run to see what it generates
exec cmd2mcpserver -dry-run -source . ./cmd2mcpserver
stdout '# Generated MCP server for cmd2mcpserver'
stdout '-- go.mod --'
stdout '-- main.go --'
stdout '-- README.md --'
stdout 'type Cmd2McpserverTool struct'
stdout '"output"'
stdout '"module"'
stdout '"server"'
stdout '"tool"'
stdout '"desc"'
stdout '"source"'
stdout '"dry-run"'
stdout '"v"'
stdout 'MCP wrapper for cmd2mcpserver command'

# Check that the generated server has all our flags
stdout '"type": "string"'
stdout '"type": "boolean"'

# Now actually generate the server (not just dry-run)
exec cmd2mcpserver -output ./server -module github.com/test/cmd2mcpserver-mcp ./cmd2mcpserver
! stderr 'error'
exists server/go.mod
exists server/main.go
exists server/bin/cmd2mcpserver

# Verify the generated go.mod has the right module name
exec cat server/go.mod
stdout 'module github.com/test/cmd2mcpserver-mcp'

# Verify the main.go has correct structure
exec cat server/main.go
stdout 'type Cmd2McpserverTool struct'
stdout 'func.*Execute.*context.Context'

-- cmd2mcpserver.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	output := flag.String("output", "", "Output directory")
	module := flag.String("module", "", "Module name")
	server := flag.String("server", "", "Server name")
	tool := flag.String("tool", "", "Tool name")
	desc := flag.String("desc", "", "Description")
	source := flag.String("source", "", "Source directory")
	dryRun := flag.Bool("dry-run", false, "Dry run mode")
	verbose := flag.Bool("v", false, "Verbose")
	flag.Parse()

	if flag.NArg() < 1 {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Usage: cmd2mcpserver [flags] <binary>\n")
		flag.PrintDefaults()
		os.Exit(1)
	}

	fmt.Printf("Converting %s\n", flag.Arg(0))
}

-- go.mod --
module test

go 1.22
